notes: Tested the analog against three hostile lenses. (1) 'This is just survivorship bias / opportunism dressed up' — false; the verse explicitly requires the opportunity to be (a) role-aligned and (b) genuinely dharmic, not just lucky-looking. (2) 'No senior engineer turns down a fortuitous opportunity' — observably false; imposter-recoil before a long-awaited project is a common pattern, and the auth-rewrite-after-incident scenario is a real instance pattern. (3) 'The named tools (Express, Redis, JWT, Sentry, Postgres, gRPC, Datadog, OAuth) are decorative' — false; they form the realistic shape of an auth-service rewrite, including the migration runbook detail. The doctrinal claim — that fortuitous arrival is itself information about the moment — is a real and useful engineering distinction. The falsifiability and counter_example narrow the claim from 'every unsolicited opportunity is svarga-dvāra' (which would be useless) to 'role-aligned, genuinely dharmic, unmanoeuvred' (which is testable). PASS.
